---
title: "Decision 3 · GTM Evidence Gate"
subtitle: "What evidence is required before committing to a go-to-market strategy?"
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Decisions <span class="sep">›</span> GTM Evidence Gate
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**This page supports:** what must be true before we commit resources to a go-to-market motion?
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">Register-driven · D3</span>
<span class="pc-next">[Review Claims & Confidence](../evidence/claims-confidence.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

The decision record below reads live from `data/sample/decision_register.csv`; its claims read from
`data/sample/claim_register.csv`.

```{python}
#| label: decision-record
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
_root = pathlib.Path.cwd()
while _root != _root.parent and not (_root / "src").is_dir():
    _root = _root.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))
from IPython.display import HTML
from src.registers import render_decision_record, render_claim_cards
HTML(render_decision_record("D3"))
```

## The gate, in one line

This is a **meta-decision**: it does not pick an option, it sets the rule for when Decisions 1 and 2
(plus a defensible price) are allowed to become commitments. The bar is **grade B** on each.

## Claims behind this decision

```{python}
#| label: claims
#| echo: false
HTML(render_claim_cards("D3"))
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false}
**Go deeper.** The threshold logic and limitations are in
[Working Paper › Go-to-Market](../working-paper/07-go-to-market.qmd). Progress is tracked on
[Claims & Confidence](../evidence/claims-confidence.qmd).
:::
